title: 🚨 Pipeline: #170 failed after 3 attempts
body:
## Pipeline Failure Report

Issue **#170** — simplify manual addition of sleep and awake has **failed 3 times** and will no longer be retried.

### Last Error
```
impl-core failed (exit 143). Log output:

```

Manual investigation required.

---
_Auto-created by pipeline.sh_
enhanced_description:
Pipeline failure report for issue #170 ("simplify manual addition of sleep and awake").

What was reported:
  The impl-core phase exited with code 143 (SIGTERM — process was killed) three consecutive times while processing issue #170. pipeline.sh auto-created this report after exhausting all retries.

Area affected:
  bot/pipeline.sh — specifically the impl-core phase and its timeout/retry handling.

Root cause hypothesis:
  Exit 143 = SIGTERM = the impl-core Claude CLI process was killed before it finished. Likely causes:
    1. Issue #170 (sleep/awake simplification) was too large/complex for the impl-core time budget.
    2. A system-level timeout (e.g., launchd watchdog, ulimit) killed the process.
    3. The Claude CLI hit a rate-limit or network error that resulted in a hung process that was then killed.

Expected behaviour:
  impl-core should either complete within the timeout or fail gracefully with a meaningful error. Three silent SIGTERM failures should trigger diagnostic output (log the last N lines of impl-core output before exit).

Current behaviour:
  impl-core exits 143 with empty log output ("Log output:" section is blank), making it impossible to diagnose the failure.

Edge cases to handle:
  - Issue #170 may need to be auto-split and re-queued (similar to Step 3.5 splitting logic).
  - The timeout budget may need to be increased, or impl-core may need a heartbeat/progress check.
  - Distinguish between SIGTERM-from-timeout vs SIGTERM-from-external-kill in the failure report.

Acceptance criteria:
  1. pipeline.sh captures and logs the last 50 lines of impl-core output before it dies (even on SIGKILL/SIGTERM).
  2. Failure report includes the captured log snippet so developers can diagnose the root cause.
  3. Optionally: automatically re-queue the failed issue as a split if exit was 143 (timeout-likely).

Testing:
  - Manually simulate an impl-core timeout by setting a very short timeout and verify the failure report now contains log output.
  - Verify the pipeline continues processing other issues after a SIGTERM failure.
implementation_notes:
WHAT YOU ACTUALLY DID:
- Files changed: bot/pipeline.sh
  1. run_claude_phase(): added PHASE_TIMED_OUT flag; on timeout, snapshot log to ${PHASE_LOG}.pre_kill before killing the process, then copy to ${PHASE_LOG}.captured after kill (pre-kill snapshot has more content since post-kill buffers may not flush)
  2. impl-core failure handler: replaced tail-30-of-log with tri-source capture (captured > log > JSON stdout), increased to 50 lines, added empty-log fallback message, annotated exit 143 as SIGTERM with timeout duration
- Approach taken: The root cause is that SIGTERM/SIGKILL prevents flushing buffered output to the log file. By snapshotting the log before sending the kill signal, we preserve whatever was written up to that point. The .captured file is checked first on failure.
- Testing done: bash -n pipeline.sh (syntax OK); full npm run test (643/643 passed); regression.cjs (21/21 passed); npm run type-check; npm run build
- Any manual steps needed: none — changes take effect on the next pipeline run
